message:
Fix EventParser JSONL async iterator missing ptScan after reset

After stream_reset_for_next + ptReset in the JSONL async iterator,
remaining incomplete bytes were never scanned by ptScan. This caused
the path tracker state (depth, keys, etc.) to be wrong for subsequent
feed() calls, dropping values and path events when a JSONL boundary
split a value across chunks.

Two issues fixed:
1. Remaining bytes after reset not scanned by ptScan (main bug)
2. seeker.reset() incorrectly put seeker back in SEEKING mode between
   JSONL values, causing subsequent non-JSON-start bytes (like digits)
   to be eaten as text instead of passed to the stream

Added resetAndScan helper that properly scans remaining bytes after
each JSONL reset, matching createParser.resetForNext() behavior.
